session.name: MPI-197 long-video stage-gap — full pod-side elimination via stock-template + bare-launch + exact-payload replays

goal.original: Find why the LTX stage-1->stage-2 gap scales with video length on remote (app-driven) pods: warm 5s video = 45s gap, 10s video = 111s gap, while short gens are fine (MPI-194 hot-store shipped).
goal.summary: Eleven pod-side axes tested in one day, ALL ACQUITTED. The exact app payload replayed through the wrapper on our image runs warm 10s/704x1216 in ~120s TOTAL (vs the app's reported 111s GAP alone). Remaining suspect: the app-driven flow itself — preview/event relay backpressure between wrapper and app, or app-side event lag inflating the perceived gap.

completed: Stock RunPod template (runpod/comfyui:cuda13.0, 5090): warm gaps 12s@5s / 22s@10s vs ours 45/111 — proves not aimdo-inherent-on-32GB
Flipped stock to our exact pins one axis at a time: ComfyUI v0.27.0@bb131be9 (21s), + --preview-method taesd (19s), + all 7 node packs at node_lock pins (19s) — all acquitted
Manual pod from OUR image (madponyinteractive/cubric-vision-pod:v0.12.0-cu130, 125GB RAM): bare stock-style launch on 8189 = 19s gap — image + host + RAM-size acquitted
Generated API workflow file (comfy_workflows/LTX_t2v.json) vs authoring template: both 17s — workflow file acquitted
Small models (VAEs/upscaler/text-projection) on network volume at 512x960: 17s — acquitted
EXACT app payload captured from local ComfyUI /history (app gen, cancelled), 3 lora backslashes healed, replayed: bare warm 82.6s vs through-wrapper warm 87.8s — wrapper spawn+HTTP acquitted
Full res 704x1216 10s warm through wrapper: 121.6s total (models on disk), 118.3s (small models on volume) — resolution and volume-x-res acquitted warm (cold volume run 205.7s = known cold tax)
MPI-197 card updated with all four rounds; MPI-198 filed (backslash loader paths break LoRA workflows on Linux/macOS LOCAL engine — comfyController.js:1081 heal gated on isRemote())
pending: Decisive next test: real app-driven gen on an app-created pod WITH the .expose-comfy debug door; compare app-UI stage gap vs the pod's own /history execution duration (status.messages timestamps)
If UI gap >> /history duration -> event/preview pipeline convicted -> instrument wrapper WS relay + app SSE consumer for backpressure
If /history itself shows the churn -> diff app-created pod config vs today's manual pod (92GB vs 125GB host, 50GB volume, hot-store layout, --listen 127.0.0.1)
Fix candidates once convicted: preview relay decoupling / bounded queue drop-oldest; or hot-store threshold lowering (HOT_STORE_MIN_BYTES 15GB) if churn is real on app pods

task_card.title: Long-video stage-gap scales with frames (aimdo VRAM<->RAM offload on 32GB) — separate from the MPI-191 volume tax

completed: MPI-197 task.json description carries the full four-round acquittal matrix with numbers
MPI-198 card filed for the Linux/macOS local-engine backslash loader bug (release-blocker tag)
pending: start.sh line 245 hardcodes 'export CUBRIC_COMFY_ENABLED=1' — pod-env override impossible; change to ${CUBRIC_COMFY_ENABLED:-1} in mpi-ci (uncarded)
RunPod 'Edit Pod' RECREATES the container and wipes /root — document in docs/runpod-troubleshooting.md or docs/builder/

Wrapper hot-store threshold HOT_STORE_MIN_BYTES=15GB means only the 40GB transformer is disk-staged; gemma/VAEs/upscaler/loras serve from volume on real pods — acquitted warm today but relevant if app-pod churn is confirmed real
MPI-197 findings should eventually land in docs/builder/research/pod-perf-investigation.md (do NOT write there without user flow; card holds everything)

next_action.description: Terminate leftover test pods if still running (user was reminded). Then: create a real pod THROUGH THE APP, open the MPI-192 debug door (.expose-comfy marker on the volume, gitignored — see docs/runpod docs), run a real app-driven 10s 704x1216 LTX gen, note the app-UI stage-1->stage-2 gap, then fetch that pod's ComfyUI /history entry (GET /wrapper/history/{prompt_id} with Bearer CUBRIC_TOKEN, or direct 8188 /history via the door) and compare execution_start->execution_success duration vs the UI-perceived gap. Disagreement = event/preview pipeline convicted; agreement = diff that pod's config vs today's manual pod.

key_decisions: One-axis-per-flip discipline on live pods instead of multi-variable guesses — this is what produced clean acquittals
Captured the EXACT app payload from the LOCAL engine's ComfyUI /history (injection code is engine-shared) instead of enumerating injection params by eye
Replays submitted via wrapper's own /wrapper/prompt endpoint (Bearer token) to include the wrapper HTTP path in the test
Kept MpiClearVram and all workflow structure identical in replays — only seed (node 77) and width/height (nodes 88/89) were varied
constraints: ComfyUI full-graph cache: re-running an identical payload returns in 0.2s — ALWAYS bump seed (prompt node '77' inputs.int) between replays
Wrapper comfy binds 127.0.0.1 on production pods — reach it via /wrapper/* endpoints with 'Authorization: Bearer <CUBRIC_TOKEN>', or open the .expose-comfy door
Killing the wrapper's ComfyUI kills the container (supervisor os._exit safety contract) — use POST /wrapper/restart-comfy instead
RunPod web terminal + direct-TCP SSH need agent/sshd our product image lacks; basic ssh.runpod.io gateway works when the account SSH key existed BEFORE pod creation
RunPod proxy only routes ports in the exposed-HTTP list; Edit Pod to add a port RECREATES the container (wipes /root, models need re-download)
Local gens produce Windows backslash lora_name values — heal to forward slashes before POSTing to any Linux ComfyUI (scratchpad/heal_replay.py does this)
Stock-pod tests ran at 512x960 to save money; original 111s app measurements were at 704x1216 — always match res when comparing

Test-pod replay tooling lives in the OLD session scratchpad (C:/Users/Fabio/AppData/Local/Temp/claude/c--AI-Mpi-Cubric-Vision/1efd9eb0-d9a9-4e90-8b54-b0963df2ad12/scratchpad/): app_payload.json, replay_body*.json, heal_replay.py — copy them out if needed, scratchpads are session-scoped

resume_prompt: Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/60478fad-7ce7-4749-a799-310a17e5c05b.json and use mpi-continue to resume MPI-197 (long-video stage-gap). All eleven pod-side axes were acquitted on 2026-07-05 via one-axis-per-flip tests and exact-payload replays (matrix + numbers in the task card). The next action: create a pod through the app with the .expose-comfy debug door, run a real app-driven 10s 704x1216 LTX gen, and compare the app-UI stage-1->stage-2 gap against the pod ComfyUI /history execution duration — disagreement convicts the preview/event pipeline, agreement means diffing the app-pod config vs the manual test pod. Always bump the seed between replays (full-graph cache returns 0.2s otherwise).
